introduction:
In Andhra Pradesh, the journey to becoming a certified driver is a two-step process: obtaining your Learner's Licence (LLR) and graduating to a Permanent Driving Licence (DL).

The AP Transport Department offers citizens the capability to apply seamlessly through the AP e-Pragathi portal or the central Sarathi portal. For LL, aadhaar-based Faceless service is available.
quickFacts:
Crucial Path
  • Stage 1 (LLR): Test taken from home via webcam if using Aadhaar authentication. Valid for 6 months.
  • Stage 2 (DL): Mandatory waiting period of 30 days.
  • Physical driving skill test before the Motor Vehicle Inspector is mandatory for DL.
documentsRequired:
Documents Required

For Learner's Licence (LLR)
  • Aadhaar Card (For eKYC)
  • Age Proof (Birth Certificate / Class X mark sheet)
  • Address Proof (Aadhaar / Voter ID)
  • Form 1 (Physical Fitness)
For Permanent DL
  • Original Learner's Licence
  • Application Form 4
  • Fee Payment Receipt & Test Slot confirmation
  • 3 Passport size photos (If offline application)
onlineApplication:
How to Apply Online

Step 1: Get Your Learner's Licence (LLR)
  1. Visit sarathi.parivahan.gov.in or the AP e-Pragathi portal.
  2. Select Andhra Pradesh and click "Apply for Learner Licence".
  3. Submit via Aadhaar Authentication to bypass the RTO visit.
  4. Pay the required fee online and watch the mandatory Road Safety Tutorial Video.
  5. Take the online written LLR test. Download your LLR immediately upon passing.
Step 2: Get Your Permanent Driving Licence (DL)
  1. After 30 days of LLR issuance, go back to the Parivahan portal.
  2. Click "Apply for Driving Licence".
  3. Validate your LLR number.
  4. Pay the DL Smart Card fee and Test fee (approx ₹700).
  5. Book a Driving Test Slot at your local AP RTO.
offlineApplication:
How to Apply Offline / Driving Test

The driving skill test is a mandatory offline event:

  1. Reach the AP Transport test track 30 minutes before your slot.
  2. Carry your original LLR, Aadhaar, and test appointment slip.
  3. You must sit for the driving test in the respective class of vehicle you applied for.
  4. Successfully clear the 8-track (for 2-wheelers) or H-track/Reverse test (for 4-wheelers).
afterApplying: Once Approved: The physical Smart Card Driving Licence will be shipped to your Aadhar address digitally verified within two to three weeks via Speed Post.
helpfulLinks: MoRTH Sarathi Portal AP Transport Dept
